name: 1Password
description: Proprietary tool for 1Password password manager.

name: 1Password
description: Proprietary graphical user interface for 1Password password manager.

name: AFICK
description: Security tool that allows to monitor the changes on your file systems, and so can detect intrusions.

name: age
description: A simple, modern and secure encryption tool (and library) with small explicit keys, no config options, and UNIX-style composability.

name: AIDE
description: File and directory integrity checker.

name: airgeddon
description: Multi-use bash script to audit wireless networks

name: Argon2
description: Key derivation function (KDF) for secure password hashing, resistant to brute-force and side-channel attacks.

name: Arpwatch
description: Tool that monitors ethernet activity and keeps a database of Ethernet/IP address pairings.

name: Authenticator
description: Open source, two-factor authentication application built for GNOME.

name: bettercap
description: Swiss army knife for network attacks and monitoring.

name: betterlockscreen
description: i3lock-color wrapper. Betterlockscreen allows you to cache images with different filters and lockscreen with blazing speed.

name: Bitwarden
description: Open source password manager with desktop, mobile, browser, and CLI versions. Cloud or self-hosted.

name: Bitwarden
description: Open source password manager with desktop, mobile, browser, and CLI versions. Cloud or self-hosted.

name: ccrypt
description: A command-line utility for encrypting and decrypting files and streams based on Rijndael.

name: cfv
description: Tiny utility to both test and create checksum files, support .sfv, .csv, .crc, .md5, md5sum, sha1sum, .torrent, par, and .par2 files.

name: Chipsec
description: a framework for analyzing platform level security of hardware, devices, system firmware, low-level protection mechanisms, and the configuration of various platform components.It contains a set of modules, including simple tests for hardware protections and correct configuration, tests for vulnerabilities in firmware and platform components, security assessment and fuzzing tools for various platform devices and interfaces, and tools acquiring critical firmware and device artifacts.

name: Cinnamon Screensaver
description: Screen locker for the Cinnamon desktop.

name: ClamAV
description: Open source antivirus engine for detecting trojans, viruses, malware & other malicious threats.

name: ClamTk
description: Graphical front-end for ClamAV using Perl and Gtk libraries. It is designed to be an easy-to-use, lightweight, on-demand antivirus scanner for Linux systems.

name: Collision
description: GUI tool to generate, compare and verify MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512, Blake3, CRC32 and Adler32 hashes.

name: darkstat
description: Captures network traffic, calculates statistics about usage, and serves reports over HTTP.

name: Deepin Screensaver
description: A lightweight Qt5 based screensaver.

name: doas
description: A portable version of OpenBSD's doas command, known for being substantially smaller in size compared to sudo while pursuing the same goal of running commands as root or another user.

name: dsniff
description: Collection of tools for network auditing and penetration testing.

name: EtherApe
description: Graphical network monitor for Unix modeled after etherman. Featuring link layer, IP and TCP modes, it displays network activity graphically. Hosts and links change in size with traffic. Color coded protocols display.

name: Ettercap
description: Multipurpose Network sniffer/analyser/interceptor/logger.

name: Figaro's Password Manager 2
description: GTK2 port of Figaro's Password Manager with some new enhancements.

name: GNOME Network Tools
description: GNOME interface for various networking tools.

name: GnuPG
description: The GNU project's complete and free implementation of the OpenPGP standard as defined by RFC4880. Free and Open Source replacement of PGP, mostly used for digital signing of packages.

name: gopass
description: Advanced console based password manager, supporting GnuPG and other backends.

name: GPG-Crypter
description: Graphical front-end to GnuPG (GPG) using the GTK3 toolkit and GPGME library.

name: GtkHash
description: A GTK utility for computing message digests or checksums

name: gtklock
description: GTK-based lockscreen for Wayland.

name: Hash-o-matic
description: Simple hash validator allowing to compare two files, generate the checksum of a file and verify if a hash matches a file.

name: Hashcat
description: Multithreaded advanced password recovery utility.

name: hashdeep
description: A cross-platform tools to compute hashes, or message digests, for any number of files

name: Himitsu
description: Secret storage manager.

name: Hostsblock
description: A script that downloads, sorts, and compiles multiple ad- and malware-blocking hosts files.

name: hping
description: Command-line oriented TCP/IP packet assembler/analyzer.

name: i3lock
description: A simple screen locker. Provides user feedback and uses PAM authentication. The background can be set to an image or solid color.

name: i3lock-blur
description: Fork of i3lock which can use your desktop with the blur effect applied as a background.

name: i3lock-color
description: Fork of i3lock with color and positioning configuration support and can use your desktop with the blur effect applied as a background.

name: IPTraf
description: Console-based network monitoring utility.

name: jnettop
description: top-like console network traffic visualizer.

name: John the Ripper
description: Password cracker.

name: justniffer
description: Network protocol analyzer that captures network traffic and produces logs in a customized way, can emulate Apache web server log files, track response times and extract all "intercepted" files from the HTTP traffic.

name: KDE Wallet Manager
description: Tool to manage the passwords on your system. By using the KDE wallet subsystem, it not only allows you to keep your own secrets but also to access and manage the passwords of every application that integrates with the wallet. Part of kde-utilities.

name: KeePass Password Safe
description: Mono-based password manager, which helps you to manage your passwords in a secure way.

name: KeePassC
description: Curses-based password manager compatible to KeePass v.1.x.

name: KeePassXC
description: Community fork of KeePassX with more active development. Compatible with KeePass v.1.x (import only) and KeePass v.2.x.

name: KeePassXC
description: Mainly used for password management and generation, but also supports attaching arbitrary files to entries in an encrypted database.

name: KeepSecret
description: Password manager for viewing, editing, creating, or deleting passwords, suitable for both desktop and mobile platforms.

name: Key Rack
description: Password manager for GNOME to view and edit keys, like passwords or tokens, stored by apps.

name: Keybase
description: Key directory mapping social media identities, with cross platform encrypted chat, cloud storage, and git repositories.

name: Keyoxide ASPE CLI
description: Ariadne Signature Profile Exchange (ASPE) CLI for identity profile platforms like Keyoxide.

name: Keysign
description: GTK application to use GnuPG for signing other people's keys. Quickly, easily, and securely.

name: Keysmith
description: OTP generation software by KDE. Part of kde-utilities.

name: KGpg
description: Simple interface for GnuPG, for KDE. Part of kde-utilities.

name: Kismet
description: 802.11 layer2 wireless network detector, sniffer, and intrusion detection system.

name: Kleopatra
description: Certificate Manager and Unified Crypto GUI for KDE. It supports managing X.509 and OpenPGP certificates in the GpgSM keybox and retrieving certificates from LDAP servers. Part of kde-pim.

name: LastPass
description: Hosted password manager.

name: Light-locker
description: A simple locker (forked from gnome-screensaver) that aims to have simple, sane, secure defaults and be well integrated with the desktop while not carrying any desktop-specific dependencies. It relies on LightDM for locking and unlocking your session via ConsoleKit/UPower or logind/systemd.

name: LinSSID
description: Graphical wireless scanner.

name: Linux Malware Detect
description: Malware scanner designed around the threats faced in shared hosted environments.

name: Logwatch
description: Customizable log analysis system.

name: Lynis
description: Security and system auditing tool to harden Unix/Linux systems.

name: MassHash
description: A set of file hashing tools (both CLI and GTK GUI) written in Python. Supported algorithms include MD5, SHA-1, SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512.

name: MATE Screensaver
description: Screensaver and locker for MATE Desktop Environment.

name: Metasploit Framework
description: An advanced open-source platform for developing, testing, and using exploit code.

name: minisign
description: Simple program that only implements key signing.

name: Nemesis
description: Command-line network packet crafting and injection utility.

name: Nessus
description: Comprehensive vulnerability scanning program.

name: Net Activity Viewer
description: Graphical network connections viewer, similar in functionality with Netstat.

name: netsniff-ng
description: High performance Linux network sniffer for packet inspection.

name: ngrep
description: grep-like utility that allows you to search for network packets on an interface.

name: Nmap
description: Security scanner used to discover hosts and services on a computer network, thus creating a "map" of the network.

name: Ntop
description: Network probe that shows network usage in a way similar to what top does for processes.

name: OpenSSH
description: De facto SSH implementation that supports message signing.

name: OpenVAS
description: Framework of several services and tools offering a comprehensive and powerful vulnerability scanning and vulnerability management solution. FOSS Nessus fork.

name: opgpcard
description: CLI tool to inspect, manage and use OpenPGP cards.

name: OSSEC
description: Open Source Host-based Intrusion Detection System that performs log analysis, file integrity checking, policy monitoring, rootkit detection, real-time alerting and active response.

name: OTPClient
description: Highly secure and easy to use GTK software for two-factor authentication that supports both Time-based One-time Passwords (TOTP) and HMAC-Based One-Time Passwords (HOTP).

name: OWASP ZAP
description: Web application security scanner.

name: Parchive
description: Utility which creates and uses PAR2 files to detect damage in data files and repair them if necessary.

name: pass
description: Simple console-based password manager featuring flat text file organization and GnuPG encryption.

name: passage
description: A fork of password-store that uses age as backend.

name: Passbook
description: Modern password manager for GNOME.

name: passphrase2pgp
description: Reproducibly generate private key in OpenPGP/OpenSSH formats according to user input passphrase and optionally sign message in one go.

name: Password Safe
description: Simple and secure password manager.

name: physlock
description: Screen and console locker.

name: pkexec
description: A Polkit application that allows an authorized user to run commands or an interactive shell as another user. Configured using Polkit rules.

name: Proton Pass
description: European-hosted password manager.

name: pwsafe
description: Unix command-line program that manages encrypted password databases.

name: pyNeighborhood
description: GTK-based SMB/CIFS browsing utility.

name: QtPass
description: GUI for pass, the standard unix password manager.

name: Quick Hash GUI
description: A GUI to enable the rapid selection and subsequent hashing of files (individually or recursively throughout a folder structure) text and (on Linux) disks.

name: reaction
description: A daemon that scans program outputs for repeated patterns, and takes action. A lightweight alternative to fail2ban.

name: Revelation
description: Password manager for the GNOME desktop.

name: RHash
description: Utility for verifying hash sums (SFV, CRC, etc). Supports lots of algorithms.

name: Rootkit Hunter
description: Checks machines for the presence of rootkits and other unwanted tools.

name: rsop
description: Stateless OpenPGP (SOP) based on rpgp.

name: run0
description: An alternative invocation of systemd-run to temporarily and interactively acquire elevated or different privileges. Does not rely on SetUID and SetGID. Uses Polkit for authentication.

name: Samhain
description: Host-based intrusion detection system (HIDS) provides file integrity checking and log file monitoring/analysis, as well as rootkit detection, port monitoring, detection of rogue SUID executables, and hidden processes.

name: scrypt
description: Command-line encryption utility featuring the memory-hardened scrypt key derivation function.

name: Seahorse
description: GNOME application for managing encryption keys and passwords in the GNOME Keyring. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Seahorse
description: GNOME application for managing encryption keys and passwords in the GNOME Keyring. Part of gnome-extra.

name: Secrets
description: Password manager for GNOME which makes use of the KeePass v.4 format.

name: sflock
description: Simple screen locker utility for X, based on slock. Provides a very basic user feedback.

name: shig
description: CLI utility to create and verify signatures in ssh-keygen compatible format.

name: slock
description: Very simple and lightweight X screen locker. Offers only a black background when locked, there are no animations or text fields.

name: Smb4K
description: Advanced network neighborhood browser and Samba share mounting utility for KDE.

name: Snort
description: Network intrusion prevention and detection system.

name: Spectools
description: A set of utilities for spectrum analyzer hardware including Wi-Spy devices.

name: sq
description: A command-line frontend for Sequoia, an implementation of OpenPGP.

name: sqop
description: Implementation of the Stateless OpenPGP Interface using Sequoia.

name: ssh-audit
description: SSH configuration auditing.

name: Sshguard
description: Daemon that protects SSH and other services against brute-force attacks, similar to Fail2ban.

name: stegcloak
description: CLI steganography tool to hide encrypted messages in plain text using invisible characters and passwords.

name: steghide
description: A steganography utility that is able to hide data in various kinds of image and audio files.

name: su
description: Command used to assume the identity of another user on the system.

name: sudo
description: Command to delegate the ability to run commands as root or another user while providing an audit trail.

name: sudo-rs
description: A memory safe implementation of sudo and su.

name: Suricata
description: High performance Network IDS, IPS and Network Security Monitoring engine.

name: swaylock
description: Screen locker for Wayland.

name: swaylock-effects
description: Swaylock, with fancy effects.

name: Tcpdump
description: Common console-based packet analyzer that allows the user to intercept and display TCP/IP and other packets being transmitted or received over a network.

name: Tiger
description: Security tool that can be used both as a security audit and intrusion detection system.

name: tpm
description: Tiny password manager, inspired by pass, written entirely in POSIX shell.

name: Tripwire
description: Intrusion detection system.

name: tsscreenlock
description: Screen locker used in theShell. Shows music controls, and if used with theShell, also shows desktop notifications.

name: Vault
description: Proprietary tool for managing secrets.

name: vlock
description: TTY locker. A mirror of the original vlock is available at github.

name: vnStat
description: Console-based network traffic monitor that keeps a log of network traffic for the selected interfaces.

name: What IP
description: Small GTK application to get info on your IP.

name: Wireshark
description: Network protocol analyzer that lets you capture and interactively browse the traffic running on a computer network.

name: xfce4-screensaver
description: A screen saver and locker that aims to have simple, sane, secure defaults and be well integrated with the xfce desktop.

name: xlockmore
description: Simple X11 screen lock with PAM support.

name: Xplico
description: Network forensics analysis tool (NFAT), which is a software that reconstructs the contents of acquisitions performed with a packet sniffer.

name: XScreenSaver
description: Screen saver and locker for the X Window System.

name: XSecureLock
description: X11 screen lock utility designed with the primary goal of security.

name: xtrlock
description: Very lightweight X display locker. Keeps windows visible and displays lock icon instead of mouse cursor. Typing password followed by enter unlocks the screen.

name: Zeek
description: Powerful network analysis framework that is much different from the typical IDS you may know.

name: zxcvbn
description: Password strength estimator inspired by password crackers.
